channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A rare winter storm is bringing heavy snow, sleet and ice to Texas and the northern Gulf Coast
item.description: A rare winter storm charging through Texas and the northern Gulf Coast on Tuesday has closed highways and airports and prompted the first blizzard warning for southeast Texas and southwest Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Costco workers authorize nationwide strike
item.description: Thousands of Costco workers nationwide voted to authorize a strike if a contract is not reached by the end of the month.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: Here's why a Buffalo Bills fan ran 17 miles to get to the game
item.description: Buffalo Bills fans show their support for the team in all sorts of ways, and for one fan it's by going the distance.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Minkewicz, NewsNation

item.title: Winds picking up in Southern California and at least 2 new fires break out
item.description: Winds picked up on Tuesday in Southern California and at least a couple of new wildfires broke out as firefighters remained on alert in extreme fire weather two weeks after two major blazes started that are still burning in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: At least 66 dead, 51 injured in a hotel fire at a ski resort in northwestern Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire at a hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey on Tuesday killed at least 66 people, Turkey's Interior Minister said. Ali Yerlikaya said at least 51 other people were injured in the disaster. “We are in deep pain.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump returns to power
item.description: President Trump took office again Monday, completing a remarkable political comeback and becoming only the second person to serve nonconsecutive terms as commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KRON4

item.title: Bay Area tech CEOs get front-row seats at Trump inauguration
item.description: (KRON) -- The biggest tech leaders of Silicon Valley got a front-row seat at President Donald Trump's inauguration on Monday. There has been a sudden support this administration has gained from the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: Sara Stinson, KRON4

item.title: Trump wants to pull the US out of the World Health Organization again. Here's what may happen next
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S. from the World Health Organization for the second time in less than five years.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cheng And Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 250 roosters, guns, cash seized in Southern California cockfighting bust
item.description: (KTLA) - Authorities in San Bernardino County arrested two men, detained 33 others and seized more than 200 fighting roosters, U.S. currency and dozens of firearms, officials announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump rolls out his blueprint on border security, but his orders will face challenges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump rolled out a blueprint to beef up security at the southern border in a series of executive orders that began taking effect soon after his inauguration Monday, making good on his defining political promise to crack down on immigration and marking another wild
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, GISELA SALOMON and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Authorities say a US border patrol agent was fatally shot in Vermont south of border
item.description: COVENTRY, Vt. (AP) — A U.S. Border Patrol agent was fatally shot Monday on a highway in northern Vermont south of the Canadian border, authorities said. The death was confirmed by the FBI and Benjamine Huffman, acting secretary of the Department of Homeland Security in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump grants sweeping pardon of Jan. 6 defendants, including rioters who violently attacked police
item.description: President Donald Trump has pardoned, commuted the prison sentences or vowed to dismiss the cases of all of the 1,500-plus people charged with crimes in the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer And Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump completes historic comeback, vowing to turn around nation in decline
item.description: President Trump completed a monumental political comeback on Monday, taking the oath of office for a second time and vowing to turn around a nation he insisted was in decline.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Residents evacuated as brush fire threatens homes near San Diego
item.description: Several residents in a San Diego area community are being evacuated as firefighters work to battle a wind-driven brush fire that is threatening homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man parachutes off TV tower in Weld County
item.description: "In case you didn't know, jumping from a TV tower is not only incredibly dangerous, but it is trespassing," the sheriff's office stated.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: Ramaswamy won't serve on Trump's DOGE as he mulls run for Ohio governor
item.description: Ramaswamy, who sought the Republican nomination for president in 2024, has signaled plans to run for governor of Ohio.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Former Planned Parenthood president, women's rights activist Cecile Richards has died at 67
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — Cecile Richards, a national leader for abortion access and women’s rights who led Planned Parenthood for 12 tumultuous years, has died. She was 67. Richards died Monday at home in New York “surrounded by family and her ever-loyal dog, Ollie,” her family said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Visually impaired Las Cruces artist turns chaotic images into abstract art
item.description: LAS CRUCES, New Mexico (KTSM) — George Mendoza, a well-known visually impaired artist from Las Cruces, has spent much of his life turning visions of chaotic colors into abstract paintings. “I don't see darkness at all. I see colors.
item.dc:creator: Jesus Baltazar, NewsNation

item.title: Search for two teens at Ke Iki beach extends to second day
item.description: Loved ones said the two Mililani teens, 17 and 18, did not return home after going on a date on Friday, Jan. 17 night.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cervantes, NewsNation

item.title: 5 highlights from Trump's inauguration speech
item.description: President Trump’s formal inauguration speech laid out his vision for America, promising an ambitious agenda and a flurry of executive actions.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MLK Day at King's Atlanta congregation features daughter's warning about anti-woke rhetoric
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A massive 70-member choir belted out “Hallelujah” at a Martin Luther King Jr. Day service Monday at his former congregation in Atlanta, followed by a stern message from his youngest daughter warning against anti-woke rhetoric.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Acting FBI Director Paul Abbate retires just minutes before Trump takes office: report
item.description: Acting FBI Director Paul Abbate, who took the helm from Christopher Wray, resigned Monday around when President Trump took his oath of office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden commutes sentence of Native American activist Leonard Peltier
item.description: President Joe Biden on Monday commuted the life sentence of activist Leonard Peltier, who was controversially convicted of murdering two FBI agents, within the final hour of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Carrie Underwood sings 'America the Beautiful' a cappella after apparent tech issue at inauguration
item.description: An apparent technical issue didn't stop Carrie Underwood from delivering "America the Beautiful" during the second inauguration of President Trump on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Cardinal Dolan, Franklin Graham pray at Trump’s inauguration
item.description: “Mr. President, the last four years, there were times I’m sure you thought it was pretty dark. But look what God has done,” Rev. Franklin Graham said.
item.dc:creator: Susie Pinto, NewsNation

item.title: Melania Trump hat: What people had to say about Inauguration Day fashion
item.description: Of all the things that people could be talking about on Inauguration Day, it didn't take long for people to latch onto the attire of Melania Trump, with one point of discussion taking the lead: her hat.
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The baby names going 'extinct' in 2025, according to BabyCenter
item.description: Much like the doomed flightless dodo, baby names like “Emory,” “Jamie” or “Remy” are quickly being stamped out by the same humans who once found them so uniquely remarkable.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pledges to release files on JFK, MLK and RFK assassinations
item.description: President-elect Trump vowed Sunday that he would release long-classified government records on the assassinations of John F. Kennedy, Martin Luther King Jr. and Robert F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump inauguration to kick off 100-day rush
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to be sworn in at the Capitol on Monday, solidifying his stunning return to the White House and kicking off what is expected to be a busy 100-day rush focused on a handful of hot-button issues.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TikTok restores service for US users based on Trump's promised executive order
item.description: TikTok restored service to users in the United States on Sunday just hours after the popular video-sharing platform went dark in response to a federal ban, which President-elect Donald Trump said he would try to pause by executive order on his first day in office.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The rise
item.description: The possibility of the U.S. outlawing TikTok kept influencers and users in anxious limbo during the four-plus years that lawmakers and judges debated the fate of the video-sharing platform. The moment its fans dreaded arrived late Saturday when the platform went dark in response to a federal ban.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: These lip balm ingredients may be making your chapped lips worse, experts say
item.description: It's tempting to reach for lip balm during the dry, winter months, but it may not be helping you at all.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Gaza ceasefire takes hold and the first handover of hostages is underway
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold in the Gaza Strip on Sunday, ushering in an initial six-week period of calm and raising hopes for the release of dozens of militant-held hostages and an end to the devastating 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How Baltimore is saving lives by offering young men resources when they put down the guns
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — With his dad in prison and his mom suffering from alcoholism, Malik Grant faced abandonment and instability early on. He got used to people letting him down.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Travis Kelce makes NFL history in first game of three-peat quest
item.description: Once again, Travis Kelce has broken a record at Arrowhead Stadium after catching a pass from Patrick Mahomes.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, Fox 5
